name: Water and mills route of Guitiriz - circular through the Monte de Fontarrán
name_original: Ruta del agua y los molinos de Guitiriz – circular por el Monte de Fontarrán
region_intro: Jogging around Guitiriz offers diverse landscapes characterized by abundant waterways, including rivers and streams, and extensive forest masses of oaks, chestnuts, and pines. The region, part of the Terra Chá, features a mix of relatively flat areas and gentle ascents, providing varied running experiences. These natural features create a serene environment ideal for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Guitiriz The most popular jogging route is Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the Terra Chá region. Another top favourite among local runners is Water and mills route of Guitiriz - circular through the Monte de Fontarrán, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route follows waterways and passes traditional mills, offering a tranquil experience. Local runners also love the Bidueiras Trail, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through wooded areas, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around Guitiriz is defined by its extensive waterways, lush forests, and varied terrain, from flatlands to gentle hills. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Guitiriz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Guitiriz?
A: Guitiriz offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 18 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ensuring there's a path for every runner.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or casual runs?
A: Yes, Guitiriz has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991741121/water-and-mills-route-of-guitiriz-circular-through-the-monte-de-fontarran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water and mills route of Guitiriz - circular through the Monte de Fontarrán&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.0-mile path that follows waterways and passes traditional mills, offering a tranquil experience.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Guitiriz?
A: Absolutely. For runners seeking a more demanding experience, Guitiriz features several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower – Cemetery with a small chapel loop from Cemiterio de Insua&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 20 miles with significant elevation changes, providing a true test of endurance.

Q: Are there routes that feature the region's famous waterways and mills?
A: Guitiriz is renowned for its abundant waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991741121/water-and-mills-route-of-guitiriz-circular-through-the-monte-de-fontarran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water and mills route of Guitiriz - circular through the Monte de Fontarrán&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, taking you alongside rivers and past traditional mills, including the historic 'Los Siete Muíños' on the Forxa River, which features a natural pool.

Q: What kind of scenic views or natural landmarks can I expect on these running routes?
A: The running routes in Guitiriz offer diverse and picturesque scenery. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Terra Chá region on trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991347308/mirador-da-terra-cha-circular-through-the-penas-de-rodas-flatland-of-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño&lt;/a&gt;. Many paths also wind through lush forests of oaks, chestnuts, and pines, and along crystal-clear streams and rivers, highlighting the area's natural beauty, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobrado Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the running paths?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the area around the Parga River features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4491959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Alberte Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, a Gothic structure with a Renaissance-style fountain and a Gothic bridge. You might also encounter traditional Galician hórreos or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower&lt;/a&gt; on longer routes.

Q: Are the running routes in Guitiriz suitable for families?
A: Many of Guitiriz's running routes are suitable for families, especially the easier, flatter paths that follow riverbanks or pass through recreational areas. These trails often provide shaded sections and opportunities to enjoy nature together. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Guitiriz?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in natural areas like Guitiriz are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. The region's diverse forests and waterways provide excellent environments for running with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific trail signs for any restrictions.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Guitiriz?
A: Yes, Guitiriz offers numerous circular running routes, which are ideal for exploring without needing to retrace your steps. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991347308/mirador-da-terra-cha-circular-through-the-penas-de-rodas-flatland-of-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador da Terra Cha – circular through the Penas de Rodas – Flatland of Miño&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991741121/water-and-mills-route-of-guitiriz-circular-through-the-monte-de-fontarran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water and mills route of Guitiriz - circular through the Monte de Fontarrán&lt;/a&gt;, both designed as loops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Guitiriz?
A: Guitiriz is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions for jogging, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. The lush forests provide shade during warmer months, and the abundant waterways are always a refreshing sight. Even in winter, the trails can be enjoyed, though some paths might be wetter.

Q: How long do the running routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running routes in Guitiriz varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, an easy 3-mile route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991741121/water-and-mills-route-of-guitiriz-circular-through-the-monte-de-fontarran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water and mills route of Guitiriz&lt;/a&gt; might take around 35 minutes, while a moderate 6.3-mile trail such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991347308/mirador-da-terra-cha-circular-through-the-penas-de-rodas-flatland-of-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador da Terra Cha&lt;/a&gt; could take over an hour.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Guitiriz?
A: Runners frequently praise Guitiriz for its diverse and serene natural environment. The combination of extensive waterways, lush forests, and varied terrain—from flatlands to gentle hills—is often highlighted. The well-maintained trails and the opportunity to discover hidden gems like ancient mills and scenic viewpoints also contribute to a highly enjoyable running experience.
tips: Monumental church located in Campo da Feira, in the town of Guitiriz, built in the 20th century, designed by the architect Andrés Reboredo. For the construction of this Latin cross church, local materials such as granite or slate for the roof were used.

The Guitiriz recreational area is a large natural space on the outskirts of the town, where the fluvial pool is located in the Escádebas river.

Around this area you can see the different mills that give the area its name.

From this point a small hiking route also begins, the Ruta de Auga e da Pedra.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Lagostelle
